verdict_reasoning: EREG is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein with a stable surface residence. Web evidence confirms it is synthesized as a 169-aa TM precursor with a 21-aa transmembrane domain and stable localization to the basolateral cell surface (verified by selective cell surface biotinylation, >90% surface pool). Although it undergoes ADAM17-mediated ectodomain shedding to release a soluble ligand, the TM precursor form is constitutively present and anchored to the PM via its own transmembrane domain. The protein is delivered directly to the cell surface via its own biosynthetic sorting motifs.
